description: Source of truth for reasoning-effort levels (CLI/bridge providers: claude-code, codex). Consumed by Java backend (agent-common ReasoningEffort), Node bridge (mcp/bridge/lib/reasoningEffort.mjs), and frontend (frontend/lib/ai-providers/reasoningEffort.ts). Do NOT edit the generated files directly - edit this JSON then re-run scripts/generate-reasoning-effort.js. ReasoningEffortResolver.java (precedence logic) is hand-written and NOT generated. Per-CLI knobs: claude-code maps each level to the categorical CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL env value (claudeCodeEffort; the CLI accepts low|medium|high|xhigh|max - minimal clamps to low). The former MAX_THINKING_TOKENS budget mapping was a no-op on every adaptive-reasoning model (Fable 5, Sonnet 5, Opus 4.7+; and 4.6-family without CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1) per code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config. codex maps each level to -c model_reasoning_effort=<codexEffort> (per developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference the CLI accepts minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh - no max, so max maps to xhigh); levels flagged codexMaxOnly clamp down to the highest non-max-only level on models not matching codexMaxModelPattern. gemini-cli and mistral-vibe are deliberately ABSENT from effortProviders (verified 2026-07-02): gemini-cli exposes NO headless effort knob (no flag/env; the only path is a modelConfigs.customAliases settings alias, which is version-fragile - gemini-cli#18423 - and thinking_level errors on pre-Gemini-3 models), and mistral-vibe has no reasoning knob at all (devstral is not a reasoning model; config.toml reasoning_field_name is response parsing, not a control). The UI hides the effort control for both via effortProviders.
